review_notes.ip_and_usage_rights: Proposal and SOW confirm Oficio Taller retains ownership of architectural drawings until full payment is received; client is granted use rights for the specific project. This is established implicitly through the phase-payment structure (40/30/30) and the payment trigger language in payment_schedule. No conflicting IP claims identified.
review_notes.esignature_validity: SOW project_type_clauses includes 'esignature' clause citing NOM-151-SCFI. Electronic signature clause is present and correctly references applicable Mexican federal law. Valid for contract execution.
review_notes.project_type_compliance: project_type_clauses contains all four required standalone_residential clauses: residential_standard, client_changes, site_conditions, esignature. No HOA, hospitality, health authority, or public procurement clauses present — correct for this project type.
review_notes.coastal_site_provisions: Coastal zone permit and wind load study are documented as client responsibilities in exclusions. Design-driven change orders triggered by site condition discovery are covered by the site_conditions clause. No liability gap identified.
review_notes.collaborator_coordination: TBD landscape and interior design collaborators are referenced in both SOW and proposal. Coordination responsibility assigned to Oficio Taller; contract is between client and collaborator. No IP conflict from coordination language.
review_notes.currency_and_payment: USD currency and payment schedule are consistently stated across SOW, budget, and proposal. CLABE placeholder in payment_instructions is appropriate at this stage — must be replaced with real CLABE before contract is sent to client.
